name: DISA Security Technical Implementation Guides
description: DISA Security Technical Implementation Guides (STIGs) provide prescriptive hardening guidance for specific technologies. FrameDiff covers version-to-version changes for the top 10 most-deployed STIGs including Windows Server 2022, Windows 11, RHEL 9, Ubuntu 22.04, SQL Server 2019, IIS 10.0, Google Chrome, Firefox, Docker, and Kubernetes.

highlights: Log4Shell (Log4j) vulnerability addressed in Java and application server STIGs
PrintNightmare mitigations added to Windows STIGs
Kubernetes STIGs updated for 1.23 release (NetworkPolicy enforcement)
macOS 12 Monterey STIG initial release
Microsoft Edge (Chromium) STIG replaced legacy Edge STIG
Supply chain risk management controls added to multiple platform STIGs

changes.title: Windows Server 2019 - Print Spooler Disabled on Non-Print Servers (PrintNightmare)
changes.oldText: The Print Spooler service must be configured to prevent downloading print drivers.
changes.newText: The Windows Print Spooler service (Spooler) must be disabled on systems not functioning as print servers. For print servers, the RestrictDriverInstallationToAdministrators registry key must be set to 1 and remote printing must be disabled. This addresses CVE-2021-34527 (PrintNightmare).
changes.rationale: PrintNightmare (CVE-2021-34527) was a critical vulnerability in the Windows Print Spooler that allowed remote code execution and local privilege escalation. The STIG response was to require Spooler disable on non-print servers rather than just patching, as multiple exploitation variants were discovered after initial patches.

changes.title: RHEL 8 - Log4j Mitigation: Java Logging Library Version Pinning
changes.oldText: RHEL 8 must use approved software repositories and validated software packages.
changes.newText: RHEL 8 systems running Java applications must not use Log4j versions 2.0-beta9 through 2.14.1 (CVE-2021-44228) or 2.15.0 (CVE-2021-45046). Systems must run Log4j 2.17.1+ or 1.x (with JMSAppender disabled). Package inventories must be audited quarterly for vulnerable logging libraries.
changes.rationale: Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228, CVSS 10.0) affected virtually all Java applications using Log4j 2.x. DISA issued emergency guidance and the STIGs were updated to codify the remediation requirements including explicit version requirements and scanning requirements.

changes.title: Kubernetes - NetworkPolicy Required for All Namespaces
changes.oldText: Kubernetes network policies should be used to restrict inter-pod communication.
changes.newText: Kubernetes must have NetworkPolicy resources defined for all non-system namespaces. A default-deny ingress and egress NetworkPolicy must exist in each namespace, with explicit allow policies for required communication paths. The CNI plugin must enforce NetworkPolicy.
changes.rationale: Kubernetes 1.21+ made NetworkPolicy enforcement more robust with improved CNI support. The STIG was updated to require a default-deny posture rather than ad-hoc network policies, reflecting zero-trust network segmentation principles.

changes.title: Windows Server 2019 - Windows Defender Credential Guard Required
changes.oldText: Windows Defender Credential Guard should be enabled on supported hardware.
changes.newText: Windows Defender Credential Guard must be enabled on all Windows Server 2019 systems with UEFI firmware and TPM 2.0. The Credential Guard configuration must be set to Enabled with UEFI lock to prevent disable via registry. This prevents NTLM hash extraction from LSASS.
changes.rationale: Pass-the-hash attacks targeting LSASS remain prevalent. Credential Guard virtualizes LSASS using Hyper-V to prevent credential extraction tools (Mimikatz) from reading hashed credentials. The STIG changed this from a SHOULD to a MUST requirement as hardware support became ubiquitous.

changes.title: Microsoft Edge (Chromium) STIG - Initial Release, Replaces Legacy Edge
changes.newText: Microsoft Edge (Chromium) STIG V1R1 published. Covers 90 checks including InPrivate mode restrictions, extension allowlisting, site isolation, Safe Browsing, and enterprise policy enforcement via Microsoft Intune or Group Policy.
changes.rationale: Microsoft retired legacy Edge (EdgeHTML) in March 2021 and replaced it with Edge Chromium. A new STIG was required as the policy structure, registry paths, and security controls differ completely between the legacy and Chromium-based versions.

changes.title: Windows Server 2019 - Advanced Audit Policy: Detailed Tracking Events
changes.oldText: Windows must be configured to audit Process Creation events.
changes.newText: Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Process Creation events with command line auditing enabled (HKLM:SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Audit\ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled = 1). This provides command-line arguments for forensic investigation.
changes.rationale: Process creation logging without command line arguments is insufficient for detecting LOLBin (Living Off the Land Binary) abuse where attackers use trusted Windows binaries with malicious arguments. Command line inclusion was added as a required setting.

changes.title: RHEL 8 - AIDE File Integrity Monitoring: Weekly Scans Required
changes.oldText: RHEL 8 must use AIDE for file integrity checking.
changes.newText: RHEL 8 must have AIDE installed and configured to run at minimum weekly integrity checks via cron or systemd timer. AIDE database must be initialized and stored offline (read-only media or separate system). Results must be reviewed and deviations investigated within 24 hours.
changes.rationale: Supply chain attacks (SolarWinds, Kaseya) demonstrated the importance of file integrity monitoring for detecting trojanized binaries. The STIG was strengthened to require scheduled automated scans rather than manual or ad-hoc checks.

changes.title: Docker Enterprise - Image Signing with Docker Content Trust (DCT)
changes.oldText: Docker images must be pulled from approved registries only.
changes.newText: Docker Enterprise must have Docker Content Trust (DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1) enforced at the daemon level, requiring all images to have valid Notary signatures before deployment. Images from internal registries must be signed with organizational keys stored in HSM or Docker secrets.
changes.rationale: Supply chain attacks targeting container images (typosquatting, compromised registries) require cryptographic image verification at pull time. Docker Content Trust enforces this by requiring Notary signatures, preventing unsigned or tampered images from being deployed.

changes.title: SQL Server 2019 - Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) Required for All User Databases
changes.oldText: SQL Server databases containing sensitive data must use encryption.
changes.newText: All SQL Server 2019 user databases must have Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) enabled. TDE certificates must be backed up to a secure location separate from the data files. For databases containing CUI or PII, cell-level encryption must be applied for the most sensitive fields in addition to TDE.
changes.rationale: Data-at-rest encryption requirements were strengthened following FISMA Modernization Act updates. TDE protects against physical media theft but not against authorized database access. Cell-level encryption provides additional protection for highest-sensitivity data.

changes.title: Firefox - WebGL Restricted to Allowlisted Sites
changes.oldText: Firefox must disable WebGL or restrict its use.
changes.newText: Firefox WebGL must be disabled via the WebGL policy setting (webgl.disabled = true) in the Firefox policy template, with exceptions only for explicitly allowlisted internal sites requiring WebGL for legitimate functionality. This prevents WebGL-based fingerprinting and GPU-based side-channel attacks.
changes.rationale: WebGL has been exploited for browser fingerprinting, cryptomining, and GPU-based speculative execution attacks. The STIG moved from a recommendation to disable to a requirement, with a managed exception process for legitimate needs.

changes.title: Windows Server 2016 - EMET Requirement Removed (End of Life)
changes.oldText: Windows Server 2016 must have the Enhanced Mitigation Experience Toolkit (EMET) installed and configured.
changes.rationale: Microsoft EMET reached end of life on July 31, 2018. Windows 10/Server 2016+ include Windows Defender Exploit Guard (WDEG) which supersedes EMET functionality natively. The STIG check was removed and replaced with WDEG configuration requirements.

changes.title: RHEL 8 - VSFTPD Disable Check Removed (FTP Universally Prohibited)
changes.oldText: RHEL 8 must not have the VSFTPD service installed or enabled.
changes.rationale: The specific VSFTPD check was removed and replaced with a broader requirement prohibiting all FTP services (vsftpd, proftpd, wu-ftpd). The scope of the control was expanded rather than being service-specific, making the individual VSFTPD check redundant.

highlights: Windows Server 2022 STIG V1R1 released (initial publication)
RHEL 9 STIG V1R1 released as RHEL 8 successor
Ubuntu 22.04 STIG V1R1 released
Kubernetes STIG updated to address Pod Security Admission (PSA) replacing deprecated PSPs
PCI DSS v4.0 alignment updates across web application STIGs
TLS 1.0 and 1.1 removal hardened across IIS and Apache STIGs

changes.title: Windows Server 2022 STIG - Initial Release (V1R1)
changes.newText: Windows Server 2022 STIG published as the successor to Windows Server 2019 STIG. Initial release covers 319 checks across audit policy, security options, account policies, Windows Defender configuration, BitLocker, and network security.
changes.rationale: Windows Server 2022 introduced Secured-core server capabilities, hardware-enforced stack protection, and improved SMB encryption. A new STIG was required to address these new security features and replace Windows Server 2019 guidance.

changes.title: RHEL 9 STIG - Initial Release (V1R1)
changes.newText: RHEL 9 STIG published with 256 checks. RHEL 9 introduced significant changes including crypto-policy enforcement by default, removal of legacy hash algorithms (MD5, SHA-1), mandatory FIPS 140-2 capable packages, and nftables replacing iptables.
changes.rationale: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9, released May 2022, made breaking changes to cryptographic policy enforcement and network filtering that required a ground-up STIG rather than an update to the RHEL 8 STIG.

changes.title: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS STIG - Initial Release (V1R1)
changes.newText: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS (Jammy Jellyfish) STIG published with 230 checks. Covers AppArmor mandatory access control, UFW firewall, systemd-resolved DNS security, AIDE file integrity monitoring, and auditd configuration.
changes.rationale: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS is a DoD-approved operating system with broad deployment. The 22.04 STIG supersedes Ubuntu 20.04 guidance for new deployments and updates package names and configuration paths changed in Jammy.

changes.title: Kubernetes STIG - Pod Security Admission Replaces Pod Security Policies
changes.oldText: Kubernetes clusters must use Pod Security Policies (PSPs) to enforce security controls at the pod level, preventing privileged containers and host namespace access.
changes.newText: Kubernetes clusters must use Pod Security Admission (PSA) or a policy enforcement webhook (OPA/Gatekeeper, Kyverno) to enforce pod security standards. PSPs were deprecated in Kubernetes 1.21 and removed in 1.25.
changes.rationale: Pod Security Policies were removed from Kubernetes in v1.25 (released September 2022). The STIG was updated to reference the replacement mechanism (Pod Security Admission built-in or third-party admission controllers) to remain applicable to current cluster versions.

changes.title: IIS 10.0 STIG - TLS 1.0 and 1.1 Disabled, TLS 1.3 Added
changes.oldText: IIS 10.0 must be configured to use TLS 1.1 or higher for encrypted connections.
changes.newText: IIS 10.0 must be configured to use only TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3. TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 must be explicitly disabled via the Windows registry or via IIS Crypto tool configuration.
changes.rationale: NIST SP 800-52 Rev 2 and PCI DSS 4.0 both require disabling TLS 1.0 and 1.1 as these protocols have known weaknesses (BEAST, POODLE). The STIG was updated to align with current federal and industry cryptographic standards.

changes.title: Google Chrome STIG - Safe Browsing and Enhanced Protection Policy Updates
changes.oldText: Google Chrome must have Safe Browsing enabled to protect users from dangerous websites.
changes.newText: Google Chrome must have Safe Browsing configured to Enhanced Protection mode via policy. Standard protection is insufficient for government use environments. The SafeBrowsingProtectionLevel policy must be set to 2 (Enhanced).
changes.rationale: Google introduced Enhanced Safe Browsing in Chrome 91 which provides faster and more accurate phishing/malware detection using real-time URL checks. Standard Safe Browsing only checks URLs against a locally-downloaded list updated every 30 minutes.

changes.title: Windows Server 2022 - SMB Signing Required on All Connections
changes.oldText: Windows Server must be configured to require SMB packet signing on SMB server communications.
changes.newText: Windows Server 2022 must require SMB signing on both SMB server (receiving) and SMB client (outbound) connections. Both RequireSecuritySignature registry settings must be enabled. SMBv1 must be disabled.
changes.rationale: NTLM relay attacks using tools like Responder and ntlmrelayx can capture and relay SMB authentication tokens unless SMB signing is enforced end-to-end. Requiring signing on both client and server sides prevents these relay attacks.

changes.title: RHEL 9 - FIPS 140-3 Mode Required (Updated from FIPS 140-2)
changes.oldText: RHEL must have FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic modules enabled.
changes.newText: RHEL 9 must operate in FIPS 140-3 mode. FIPS mode must be enabled at installation time via the fips=1 kernel parameter. FIPS 140-3 modules supersede FIPS 140-2 in RHEL 9.
changes.rationale: NIST deprecated FIPS 140-2 on September 21, 2021, with a transition to FIPS 140-3. RHEL 9 ships with FIPS 140-3 validated cryptographic modules. The STIG updated validation requirements to reflect the new standard.

changes.title: Ubuntu 22.04 - UFW Default Deny with Explicit Allow Rules
changes.oldText: Ubuntu 20.04 must configure the Uncomplicated Firewall (UFW) to allow only necessary traffic.
changes.newText: Ubuntu 22.04 must configure UFW with a default deny policy for incoming and forwarded traffic, with explicit allow rules for required services. UFW must be enabled and set to start on boot. IPv6 rules must also be configured.
changes.rationale: Ubuntu 22.04 changed the default UFW configuration and added IPv6 support by default. The STIG was updated to require explicit IPv6 rules and to mandate that UFW is configured at provisioning time, not as a post-install step.

changes.title: Docker Enterprise - Rootless Mode and User Namespace Remapping
changes.oldText: Docker containers must not run as root unless explicitly required and documented.
changes.newText: Docker Enterprise must be configured with user namespace remapping enabled, mapping container root to an unprivileged host user (subuid/subgid). Alternatively, rootless Docker mode must be used. Container USER directives must specify non-root users.
changes.rationale: User namespace remapping was added to Docker to provide defense-in-depth against container escapes. If a process escapes the container, it runs as an unprivileged user on the host rather than as root. This is now considered a required control rather than best practice.

changes.title: SQL Server 2019 - Contained Database Authentication Disabled
changes.oldText: SQL Server must disable contained databases unless specifically required.
changes.newText: SQL Server 2019 must have the 'contained database authentication' server-level option set to 0 (disabled) via sp_configure. If contained databases are required, individual database owners must be explicitly documented and approved. All contained database users must use Windows Authentication.
changes.rationale: Contained databases in SQL Server allow authentication at the database level, bypassing server-level login controls. This creates a risk of privilege escalation and authentication bypass if not properly controlled. The STIG was strengthened to require explicit configuration rather than just documentation.

changes.title: Firefox STIG - Enhanced Tracking Protection and DNS-over-HTTPS Policy
changes.oldText: Firefox must have Enhanced Tracking Protection enabled.
changes.newText: Firefox must have Enhanced Tracking Protection set to Strict mode via policy. The NetworkPrediction (DNS prefetch) policy must be disabled. DNS-over-HTTPS must be disabled or configured to use agency-approved DoH resolvers only via the DNSOverHTTPS policy.
changes.rationale: DNS-over-HTTPS bypasses agency DNS filtering controls and can prevent security monitoring of DNS queries. Firefox enabled DoH by default in some configurations. The STIG was updated to explicitly address DoH governance and require Strict ETP which blocks fingerprinters in addition to trackers.

changes.title: Windows Server 2022 - PowerShell Script Block Logging Required
changes.oldText: Windows must be configured to audit PowerShell script execution.
changes.newText: Windows Server 2022 must enable PowerShell Script Block Logging (HKLM:SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\ScriptBlockLogging\EnableScriptBlockLogging = 1) and PowerShell Transcription. Module logging must also be enabled for all modules. Logs must be forwarded to SIEM.
changes.rationale: PowerShell remains the primary attack tool for living-off-the-land (LOTL) attacks. Script block logging captures the deobfuscated PowerShell code executed, which is critical for forensic analysis and SIEM alerting. The previous STIG only required basic auditing.

changes.title: Windows Server 2022 - NTLM v1 Check Removed (Superseded by Blanket NTLM Disable)
changes.oldText: Windows Server must be configured to prevent the use of NTLMv1 for authentication.
changes.rationale: The specific NTLMv1 check was removed because a higher-severity control requiring complete NTLM disable (or restriction to trusted servers only) supersedes it. Checking only for NTLMv1 while allowing NTLMv2 provided insufficient assurance.

highlights: Windows Server 2022 V2R3 to V2R4: New checks for Windows Defender for Endpoint and LAPS
RHEL 9 V1R2 to V1R3: FIPS 140-3 compliance required (up from FIPS 140-2)
Ubuntu 22.04 V1R1 to V2R1: New checks for UFW firewall and AppArmor profiles
Kubernetes V1R12 to V2R1: Pod Security Admission replaces deprecated PodSecurityPolicy
Google Chrome V2R9 to V2R10: Enhanced protection now required; new post-quantum crypto check
SQL Server 2019: New checks for Azure AD authentication and contained database mode
Docker: Seccomp profile check elevated to CAT I; new image registry restriction check
Windows 11: BitLocker updated to require XTS-AES 256; Internet Explorer checks removed

changes.title: Windows Server 2022 - Defender for Endpoint Behavioral Monitoring Must Be Enabled
changes.newText: Windows Defender for Endpoint behavioral monitoring must be enabled on Windows Server 2022 systems. The 'DisableBehaviorMonitoring' registry key under HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Real-Time Protection must be set to 0 or not exist. Finding ID: V-254487. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: Behavioral monitoring detects malicious patterns that signature-based detection misses. With fileless malware and living-off-the-land attacks becoming prevalent, behavior-based detection is now considered essential for Windows Server environments.

changes.title: Windows Server 2022 - LAPS Must Be Configured for Local Administrator Accounts
changes.newText: Windows Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) must be configured for all local administrator accounts. The LapsAdministratorAccountName policy must specify the managed account, and PasswordComplexity must be set to 4 (letters, numbers, and symbols). Finding ID: V-254512. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: Shared or static local administrator passwords allow lateral movement after a single credential compromise. LAPS rotates local admin passwords automatically, limiting the blast radius of a credential theft.

changes.title: Windows Server 2022 - SMBv1 Check Updated for PowerShell 7
changes.oldText: The check procedure used the Get-WindowsOptionalFeature PowerShell cmdlet to verify SMBv1 is disabled. The STIG required the SMB1Protocol-Server feature to be in a Disabled state.
changes.newText: Check procedure updated to use Get-SmbServerConfiguration in addition to Get-WindowsOptionalFeature, as Windows Server 2022 with PowerShell 7 may return different results. Both the feature state and the EnableSMB1Protocol registry value must now be verified. Finding ID: V-254488. CAT I (High).
changes.rationale: Inconsistency in how PowerShell 7 reports SMBv1 state was causing false-negative scan results. The updated check procedure closes a gap that allowed some SMBv1-enabled systems to pass automated scans.

changes.title: RHEL 9 - FIPS Mode: Updated to Require FIPS 140-3 Compliant Modules
changes.oldText: RHEL 9 must use FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic modules. The system must be booted with fips=1 on the kernel command line and the system crypto policy must be set to FIPS.
changes.newText: RHEL 9 must use FIPS 140-3 validated cryptographic modules. The system crypto policy must be set to FIPS or FIPS:OSPP. The fips=1 kernel parameter must be present. The openssl-libs package must be at version 3.0.7 or later. Run 'fips-mode-setup --check' to verify. Finding ID: V-257777. CAT I (High).
changes.rationale: FIPS 140-3 superseded FIPS 140-2 for new validations as of September 2021. RHEL 9's crypto stack is built on OpenSSL 3.x which supports FIPS 140-3, and the STIG now explicitly requires the newer standard.

changes.title: RHEL 9 - SSH Must Use Ed25519 or ECDSA Keys (RSA-1024 Explicitly Prohibited)
changes.newText: RHEL 9 SSH host keys must use Ed25519 or ECDSA algorithms. RSA keys shorter than 3072 bits must not be used. The 'HostKeyAlgorithms' directive in /etc/ssh/sshd_config must explicitly list only ecdsa-sha2-nistp256, ecdsa-sha2-nistp384, or ssh-ed25519. Finding ID: V-257833. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: RSA-1024 and RSA-2048 provide insufficient security against current computational capabilities. The transition to Ed25519 and ECDSA ensures forward compatibility with post-quantum security roadmaps.

changes.title: Ubuntu 22.04 - UFW Must Be Enabled and Default Policy Set to Deny Incoming
changes.newText: The Uncomplicated Firewall (UFW) must be enabled on Ubuntu 22.04 and the default incoming policy must be set to 'deny'. Verify with 'sudo ufw status verbose'. The output must show 'Status: active' and 'Default: deny (incoming)'. Finding ID: V-260552. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: Ubuntu 22.04 ships with UFW pre-installed but disabled. The new STIG checks ensure host-based firewall is active and restrictive by default, providing defense-in-depth even when network-level firewalls are bypassed.

changes.title: Ubuntu 22.04 - AppArmor Profiles Must Be Enforced for All Installed Applications
changes.newText: AppArmor profiles must be in enforce mode for all installed applications that have an available profile. Verify with 'sudo aa-status'. All profiles must show as 'enforce' mode; complain mode is not acceptable for production systems. Finding ID: V-260553. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: AppArmor provides mandatory access controls that limit what a compromised application can access. Systems with profiles in complain mode receive no protection - only logging.

changes.title: Kubernetes - Pod Security: PodSecurityPolicy Replaced by Pod Security Admission
changes.oldText: Kubernetes must use PodSecurityPolicy (PSP) admission controller to restrict pod capabilities. PSP must prohibit privileged containers and hostPath volume mounts. The PSP admission controller must be enabled in the API server.
changes.newText: Kubernetes must use Pod Security Admission (PSA) to restrict pod security contexts. PodSecurityPolicy was deprecated in Kubernetes 1.21 and removed in 1.25. Clusters must configure PSA namespace labels to enforce the 'restricted' or 'baseline' profile. The kube-apiserver must have admission-plugins set to include PodSecurity. Finding ID: V-242408. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: PSP was removed from Kubernetes 1.25. STIG updated to reflect the replacement mechanism (PSA). Organizations still using PSP on Kubernetes 1.25+ will find the entire security mechanism silently inactive.

changes.title: Kubernetes - Secrets Must Be Encrypted at Rest in etcd
changes.newText: Kubernetes secrets stored in etcd must be encrypted at rest. The API server must be configured with an EncryptionConfiguration file specifying AES-CBC or AES-GCM encryption for 'secrets' resources. Verify by base64-decoding a secret directly from etcd - it must not return plaintext. Finding ID: V-242434. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: By default, Kubernetes stores secrets in etcd in base64-encoded plaintext. Any attacker with etcd access can trivially decode all secrets. Encryption at rest is now required.

changes.title: IIS 10.0 - TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 Must Be Disabled
changes.newText: IIS 10.0 must be configured to only allow TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3. TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 must be disabled via the Windows Registry at HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols. The 'Enabled' DWORD under 'TLS 1.0\Server' and 'TLS 1.1\Server' must be set to 0. Finding ID: V-218760. CAT I (High).
changes.rationale: TLS 1.0 is vulnerable to POODLE and BEAST attacks. TLS 1.1 lacks support for modern cipher suites. Both were deprecated by the IETF in RFC 8996 (2021).

changes.title: Google Chrome - Safe Browsing Enhanced Protection Now Required
changes.oldText: Google Chrome must have Safe Browsing enabled. The SafeBrowsingEnabled policy must be set to true. SafeBrowsingProtectionLevel may be set to 1 (Standard protection) or 2 (Enhanced protection).
changes.newText: Google Chrome must have Safe Browsing Enhanced Protection enabled. The SafeBrowsingProtectionLevel policy must be set to 2 (Enhanced protection). Standard protection (value: 1) is no longer acceptable. Enhanced protection provides real-time URL checking against Google Safe Browsing. Finding ID: V-221562. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: Standard Safe Browsing relies on locally cached blocklists updated every 30 minutes, leaving a window for new phishing sites. Enhanced protection uses real-time URL lookup, significantly reducing exposure to zero-day phishing attacks.

changes.title: Google Chrome - Post-Quantum Cryptography: Kyber Key Encapsulation Must Be Enabled
changes.newText: Google Chrome must have Kyber key encapsulation enabled for TLS connections. The PostQuantumKeyAgreementEnabled policy must not be set to false. Chrome's default behavior enables Kyber (X25519Kyber768Draft00) for TLS, providing protection against 'harvest now, decrypt later' attacks using quantum computers. Finding ID: V-260901. CAT III (Low).
changes.rationale: NIST finalized post-quantum cryptography standards in 2024 (FIPS 203/204/205). Chrome's implementation of Kyber provides hybrid classical/post-quantum key exchange, protecting TLS sessions against future quantum decryption.

changes.title: SQL Server 2019 - Azure Active Directory Authentication Must Be Enabled Where Available
changes.newText: SQL Server 2019 instances accessible from Azure environments must be configured to use Azure Active Directory (Azure AD / Entra ID) authentication. SQL-only logins must be minimized and audited. Finding ID: V-214036. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: SQL Server authentication relies on database-level passwords that are frequently weak or shared. Azure AD authentication leverages enterprise identity infrastructure including MFA, Conditional Access policies, and centralized credential management.

changes.title: SQL Server 2019 - Contained Databases Must Be Disabled Unless Required
changes.oldText: SQL Server must be configured to prohibit or restrict the use of unauthorized functions, ports, protocols, and/or services. The contained database authentication feature must be evaluated per policy.
changes.newText: SQL Server contained database authentication must be disabled unless specifically required and documented. Set sp_configure 'contained database authentication' to 0. Contained databases carry their own authentication principals and can bypass Windows/AD credential controls including password complexity policies and account lockout. Finding ID: V-213955. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: Contained database authentication bypasses OS-level account policies that the STIG otherwise requires. An attacker with access to a contained database can create users that bypass enterprise security controls.

changes.title: Windows Server 2022 - Internet Explorer Enhanced Security Configuration Check Removed
changes.oldText: Internet Explorer Enhanced Security Configuration (IE ESC) must be enabled for all users on Windows Server 2022. Verify via Server Manager that IE Enhanced Security Configuration shows 'On' for Administrators and Users.
changes.rationale: Internet Explorer was retired in June 2022 and removed from Windows Server 2022 in subsequent updates. The check was removed as the underlying application no longer exists.

changes.title: RHEL 9 - Telnet Client Check Removed (Package No Longer Available)
changes.oldText: The telnet package must not be installed on RHEL 9. Verify with 'rpm -q telnet'. If the package is installed, this is a finding.
changes.rationale: The telnet client package was removed from RHEL 9's package repository. The check was removed since the package cannot be installed through standard channels.

changes.title: Docker - Runtime Seccomp Profile: Default Profile Now Mandatory, Severity Elevated to CAT I
changes.oldText: Docker must restrict containers' system calls. A seccomp profile should be applied to containers. The default Docker seccomp profile or a more restrictive profile is acceptable.
changes.newText: The default Docker seccomp profile or a more restrictive custom profile must be applied to all containers. Running containers without a seccomp profile (--security-opt seccomp=unconfined) is a CAT I finding. Inspect running containers with 'docker inspect <container> --format={{.HostConfig.SecurityOpt}}'. Finding ID: V-235834.
changes.rationale: The previous check used 'should' language that auditors treated as optional. The 2024 update changes to 'must' and elevates unconfined containers to CAT I (High) severity, since they can call arbitrary kernel syscalls.

changes.title: Docker - Container Images Must Come From Approved Registries Only
changes.newText: Docker environments must be configured to pull images only from approved, internal registries. Images pulled from public registries without review are prohibited in production environments. Finding ID: V-260811. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: Supply chain attacks through compromised public container images have increased significantly. Restricting image sources to approved registries with vulnerability scanning enforces a security review gate before images reach production.

changes.title: Firefox - DNS over HTTPS Must Be Controlled by Enterprise Policy
changes.newText: Firefox's DNS over HTTPS (DoH) setting must be controlled by enterprise policy. Either DoH must be disabled for environments using internal DNS resolvers, or it must be configured to use an approved DoH provider. Uncontrolled DoH settings can bypass enterprise DNS security controls and content filtering. Finding ID: V-251720. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: Firefox's default DoH configuration can bypass enterprise DNS security tools (secure web gateways, content filtering, threat intelligence feeds) that rely on DNS inspection.

changes.title: Windows 11 - BitLocker: XTS-AES 256 Now Required (AES-CBC 128 No Longer Acceptable)
changes.oldText: Windows 11 BitLocker must use AES-CBC 128-bit or stronger encryption. The EncryptionMethodByDriveType policy must be configured to set Operating System drives to at least AES-CBC 128.
changes.newText: Windows 11 BitLocker must use XTS-AES 256-bit encryption for operating system drives. The EncryptionMethodByDriveType policy must set OS drives to XTS-AES 256 (value: 7). AES-CBC 128-bit is no longer acceptable for OS drives in new deployments. Finding ID: V-253422. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: XTS-AES 256 provides stronger protection against cryptographic attacks on full-disk encryption. NIST and CISA guidance now recommends XTS-AES as the preferred mode for disk encryption.

changes.title: Windows 11 - Microsoft Pluton Security Processor Must Be Enabled Where Available
changes.newText: On Windows 11 systems with Microsoft Pluton security processors, the Pluton security processor must be enabled. Verify via Device Security > Security Processor Details. Pluton provides enhanced protection for cryptographic keys and Credential Guard. Finding ID: V-260901. CAT III (Low).
changes.rationale: Microsoft Pluton integrates the security processor directly on the CPU die, eliminating the attack surface of the bus between CPU and discrete TPM.

changes.title: SQL Server 2019 - SQL Server Browser Service Check Consolidated
changes.oldText: The SQL Server Browser service must be disabled if not required. Verify the SQL Server Browser service is disabled via services.msc or 'Get-Service -Name SQLBrowser'.
changes.rationale: This check was consolidated into the broader 'unnecessary services must be disabled' check (SQL6-D0-001100). The standalone check was creating confusion when SQL Browser is required for named instances in clustered configurations.

changes.title: Kubernetes - Audit Logging: RequestResponse Level Required for Sensitive Resources
changes.oldText: Kubernetes must enable audit logging. The kube-apiserver must be configured with audit-log-path and audit-policy-file. The audit policy must capture Metadata level for all resources.
changes.newText: Kubernetes audit logging must capture RequestResponse level for sensitive resources including secrets, configmaps, and roles/rolebindings. The audit policy file must specify RequestResponse for resources in the '' and 'rbac.authorization.k8s.io' API groups, and Metadata for all other resources. Audit logs must be retained for minimum 30 days online and 1 year total. Finding ID: V-242402. CAT II (Medium).
changes.rationale: Metadata-level logging for secrets only captures that a secret was accessed, not the response body. RequestResponse logging is needed to detect data exfiltration patterns.
